title: Isolepis planifolia
taxonomy: Isolepis planifolia is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family comprises a large number of species, many of which are aquatic or semi-aquatic plants. Isolepis planifolia is further classified within the genus Isolepis, which includes approximately 20 species of sedges. The genus Isolepis is part of the subfamily Cyperoideae. The classification of Isolepis planifolia is not well-documented, but it is generally considered to be a type of sedge.
uses: Isolepis planifolia, also known as the flat-stemmed club rush, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its rhizomes have been employed to treat various ailments, including rheumatism and digestive issues. In some regions, the plant's roots are used as a treatment for fever and other infections. Additionally, the plant's leaves have been used to treat skin conditions and wounds. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Isolepis planifolia for medicinal purposes.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits.
